channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ongoing Situation in the Middle East as of May 2, 2026
item.description: As of May 2, 2026, tensions and military confrontations continue in the Middle East, affecting civilians and overseas workers from the Philippines and other countries. The situation remains fluid, wit...

item.title: Authorities Manage Safety as 20,000 Attend Unauthorised Rave at French Military Site
item.description: Around 20,000 people gathered for an unauthorised rave at a military firing range near Bourges, France, despite warnings about unexploded ordnance that could pose a risk of explosion. The event, which...

item.title: Tech Layoffs May Be Driven by Costs and Overstaffing, Not Just AI
item.description: Despite the recent wave of headlines blaming artificial intelligence for layoffs at major tech companies, the reality may be more nuanced. Amazon, Google, and Meta currently employ roughly the same nu...

item.title: China Extends Zero-Tariff Policy to All African Diplomatic Partners
item.description: On 1st May 2026, China officially rolled out a zero-tariff policy covering 53 African countries with which it holds diplomatic relations, marking a significant boost in China-Africa trade ties. This i...

item.title: Adeboye advises men to consider practical skills and moderation when choosing a wife
item.description: Pastor Enoch Adeboye, the General Overseer of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG), don give advice to young men on how to choose the right partner. During the May Holy Ghost Service, he warn m...

item.title: UDSM and CRJE strengthen collaboration for better student-industry connections
item.description: The University of Dar es Salaam's College of Engineering and Technology (CoET) has partnered with CRJE (East Africa) Ltd to bridge the gap between academia and industry. The collaboration was showcase...

item.title: Mini Crossword Puzzle Challenge for Sunday, 3 May
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald presents the Superquiz for Sunday, 3 May, 2026. Readers are invited to test their knowledge with a Mini Crossword featuring ten clues across twenty-five squares. The puzzle i...

item.title: Nepean by-election seen as key test for Liberals amid strong challenge from One Nation and independent candidate
item.description: Voters in Victoria’s Mornington Peninsula headed to the polls for the Nepean by-election, a contest widely viewed as an early indicator of voter sentiment ahead of the state election scheduled for Nov...

item.title: Blues' Struggles Continue in Heavy Loss to Saints as Voss Calls for Improved Intent
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss expressed frustration with his team's lack of intent following their 39-point loss to St Kilda on Saturday night. Despite a promising first half, the Blues faltered in the t...

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney to attend European Political Community summit in Armenia
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is travelling to Armenia to participate in the European Political Community Leaders’ Summit, marking a notable step in Canada’s engagement with European partners. According ...

item.title: Bela Padilla Supports Calumpit, Bulacan Community on 'Everybody Sing' Special Round
item.description: In a recent episode of ABS-CBN’s hit musical game show 'Everybody Sing,' actress Bela Padilla took part in the ‘Singko Blanko’ special round, helping residents of Calumpit, Bulacan, showcase their sin...

item.title: Tamil Nadu 2026 Assembly Elections: Key Constituencies and Leading Candidates
item.description: Tamil Nadu concluded voting across all 234 assembly constituencies in a single phase on April 23, achieving a record voter turnout exceeding 84.8 per cent. The vote counting is scheduled for May 4, wh...

item.title: Heavy Rains in Kenya Lead to 10 Deaths and Rising Flood Risks
item.description: At least ten people have died in Kenya following heavy rainfall that caused widespread flooding and landslides across several regions. Seven of the fatalities occurred in eastern Kenya, highlighting t...

item.title: Ten individuals from a Crewe religious group released on bail following arrests
item.description: Ten individuals associated with a religious group in Crewe were released on bail after being arrested as part of an ongoing investigation. Authorities have not disclosed the specific allegations again...

item.title: US Court Blocks Trump-Era Immigration Application Halt
item.description: A federal judge in Boston has ruled against the Trump administration’s policy that slowed down immigration applications for people from countries on the travel ban list. The judge, Julia Kobick, found...

item.title: Australia invests in community language schools to boost regional fluency
item.description: Australia is facing a decline in proficiency in Asian languages, raising concerns about the country's ability to engage effectively with its fastest-growing regional neighbours. In response, the Alban...

item.title: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar Embarks on Official Visit to Jamaica, Suriname and Trinidad and Tobago
item.description: External Affairs Minister Dr S Jaishankar is undertaking a significant official visit to the Caribbean nations of Jamaica, Suriname and Trinidad and Tobago from May 2 to May 10, 2026. The main purpose...

item.title: Fight Club’s 25th Anniversary Re-Release Box Office Performance Compared to Moneyball
item.description: The iconic film Fight Club, directed by David Fincher and starring Brad Pitt and Edward Norton, celebrated its 25th anniversary with a re-release in theaters. The re-release generated excitement among...

item.title: Bishnoi gang fires at restaurant in Surrey, issues threat to Kapil Sharma
item.description: On early Saturday in Surrey, British Columbia, a shooting incident occurred at the Chai Sutta Bar, a cafe situated adjacent to Kapil Sharma’s Kap's Cafe. The Lawrence Bishnoi gang reportedly claimed r...

item.title: Shelter-in-place lifted in Behchokǫ̀ after police respond to reported gunfire and make arrests
item.description: Residents of Behchokǫ̀, Northwest Territories, were told it was safe to resume normal activities Friday evening after the RCMP lifted a shelter-in-place order that had been in effect for nearly nine h...
